data.title: Biggest stock movers: AVGO, LUNR, ZIM, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Friday as investors awaited Fed Chair Jerome Powell’s speech, following a weaker-than-expected rise in nonfarm payrolls. Market sentiment remained cautious, with volatility driven by tariff's policy uncertainties and trade tensions.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers

Biggest stock gainers

  • Walgreens Boots (NASDAQ:WBA) +5% – Shares gained after private equity firm Sycamore Partners agreed to acquire the company for $10B. Sycamore will pay $11.45 per share, with shareholders potentially receiving an additional $3 from proceeds tied to the sale of Walgreens' primary-care assets, including VillageMD. The total transaction value could reach $23.7B. Expected to close in Q4, the deal includes a 35-day “go-shop” period, allowing Walgreens to seek alternative offers. Sycamore has secured fully committed financing, and the transaction is not subject to a financing condition.
  • Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) +13% – Shares soared after the semiconductor and software giant posted strong FQ1 results and guidance, reinforcing that AI-related spending remains robust. CEO Hock Tan highlighted continued strength in AI semiconductor revenue, projecting $4.4B in Q2 as hyperscale partners ramp up investments in AI XPUs and connectivity solutions for AI data centers. For Q2, Broadcom forecasts revenue of approximately $14.9B, surpassing the $14.59B consensus, with adjusted EBITDA expected to be around 66% of total revenue.
  • The Gap (NYSE:GAP) +18% – Shares jumped as the retailer surpassed Q4 estimates, fueled by better-than-expected comparable sales in three of its four brands and its strongest gross margin in two decades. Despite the strong results, Gap issued cautious guidance for the current quarter, citing macroeconomic uncertainty and potential effects of U.S. trade policy, though less than 1% of its merchandise comes from Mexico and Canada. For Q1, net sales are projected to be flat to slightly higher than Q1 2024’s $3.4B, with a modest Y/Y gross margin increase from 41.2%. In FY25, net sales are expected to rise 1%–2% to $15.25B–$15.40B, in line with the $15.38B consensus, while operating income is forecast to be between $1.20B and $1.22B.
  • ZIM Integrated Shipping (NYSE: ZIM) +7% - Shares soared amid takeover speculation, following reports that CEO Eli Glickman is exploring a management-led buyout of the Israel-based shipping firm. According to a StreetInsider report citing an unidentified source, Glickman is working with advisers on a potential bid, though it remains unclear whether his efforts will materialize or if the board will be receptive. The company is set to report its Q4 earnings on Wednesday.

Biggest stock losers

  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) -20% – Shares plunged after the company posted mixed FQ1 results and issued a weaker-than-expected outlook. HPE also announced a cost-reduction program, including a workforce reduction of up to 3,000 employees. For the current quarter, the company expects adjusted EPS between $0.28 and $0.34, significantly below the $0.48 consensus, with projected revenue of $7.2B–$7.6B, missing the $7.94B estimate. For FY2025, HPE forecasts adjusted EPS with a midpoint of $1.80, falling short of the $2.12 consensus.
  • Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) -34% – Shares plunged after reports of complications with its IM-2 mission Nova-C lunar lander, Athena, despite maintaining a signal with the spacecraft. The company has been reallocating power to critical systems to address unexpected positioning issues on the moon, raising investor concerns about the mission's success and its broader implications. With a press conference scheduled for later today, the market awaits further details on Athena’s status and next steps. The setback has dampened optimism about Intuitive Machines’ space exploration capabilities, fueling uncertainty about its valuation and future prospects.

data.title: Airbus says January-February deliveries down from year ago
data.description:
Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) reported Friday that deliveries for January and February fell 18% Y/Y to 65 aircraft, including 40 jets handed over to customers in February, lagging behind the same two-month period in 2024 when it delivered 79 aircraft.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) had warned about relatively weak deliveries in this year's Q1 after supplies from engine maker CFM were pulled forward to last year's Q4 to help the planemaker to broadly reach its annual target.

The company also said it secured just 14 new orders for February, all for the A321neo, bringing the YTD total orders to 69; after cancellations, net orders in the first two months reached 65, up from 33 at the same point last year.

When Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) unveiled FY 2024 results, which included a net profit of €4.2B, the company guided for 2025 deliveries of ~820 aircraft, compared to 766 planes that were handed over in 2024.

data.title: Coinbase rated new Buy at Rosenblatt on Trump tailwinds
data.description:
Rosenblatt started coverage on Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) with a Buy rating in a recent note on the view that the Trump administration "will be a huge positive catalyst for crypto as long-awaited regulation will drive full-blown institutional adoption."

On Thursday evening, the White House officially established the long-awaited national bitcoin (BTC-USD) reserve, which is capitalized through bitcoin owned by the Treasury Department that were forfeited in criminal or civil asset forfeiture cases. White House AI and crypto czar David Sacks is set to lead a pioneering Trump-hosted summit on crypto, scheduled for Friday at 3:00 p.m. ET.

Analyst Chris Brendler warned that price volatility will continue to linger "when investing in this new technology." But, calling Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) "the clear blue chip in the sector," the analyst believes the exchange "should be a beneficiary of multiple positive tailwinds."

"COIN has also proven its ability to navigate both Bull and Bear markets and with the growth in nontrading revenue, we believe the stock will prove more resilient in the next "Crypto Winter," the March 6 note said.

Rosenblatt's Buy rating diverges from the SA Quant system rating of Hold and agrees with the average SA analyst rating and the average Wall Street analyst rating, both at Buy.

COIN shares gained 2% minutes after Friday's opening bell.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Dow, S&P, and Nasdaq are muted as labor report comes in lower than expected
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages dipped on Friday as nonfarm payrolls rose less than expected. 

Early on and the S&P 500 500 (SP500) was near flat, the Nasdaq  Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.24%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 3.92%.

The February nonfarm payrolls rose slightly less than expected and came in at +151K vs. +160K consensus.

U.S. stocks were slammed lower Thursday, with the S&P 500 marking its lowest close since September as pressure from tech stocks, labor market weakness, and tariff-policy uncertainty took their toll on large-cap equities.

“Fierce cold winds were again blowing in U.S. equity markets yesterday, with ongoing trade policy volatility sending the S&P 500 to its worst day of 2025 for a second time this week,” Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

"Having imposed aggressive taxes on U.S. consumers at the start of the week, US President Trump partially retreated from taxing Mexican and Canadian imports yesterday (assuming goods are compliant with the revised NAFTA). The taxes are delayed until 2 April, but markets will likely price in more retreats," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Broadcom (AVGO) shares rose 3.7% after the semiconductor and software giant reported first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations and suggested artificial intelligence spending has not slowed down.

"Today’s U.S. employment report will not really reflect government job losses because the government is not a significant employer and because timing will push the losses into later months. The main future employment hit is likely to be from private sector businesses that depend (directly or indirectly) on government employment," Donovan added. 

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is also set to speak later in the day today.

data.title: Zimmer Biomet gets FDA Clearance for its revision knee implant alternative
data.description:
• Zimmer Biomet Holdings (NYSE:ZBH) said it received the U.S. FDA 510((k)) clearance for Persona Revision SoluTion Femur.
  • Persona Revision SoluTion Femur is a revision knee implant component which is an alternative to patients with metals sensitivities.
  • The company  said that Persona Revision SoluTion Femur will be commercially available in the U.S. in Q3, 2025.

data.title: KLX Energy enters into new credit agreement to refinance existing senior secured notes due 2025
data.description:
• KLX Energy Services Holdings (NASDAQ:KLXE) on Friday said it has entered agreements to refinance its existing 2025 senior secured notes by issuing about $232 million of senior secured notes due March 2030.
  • The company also announced it has entered into a new ABL credit facility due March 2028 with a $125 million commitment, a first-in-last-out facility with a $10 million commitment, and a committed incremental loan option with a $25 million commitment.
  • The closing of the refinancing is expected to occur on or about March 11, 2025.
  • Additionally, KLX expects Q4 revenue to come in at the midpoint of previously disclosed guidance, and adjusted EBITDA margin to exceed the high-end of guidance.
  • KLXE -3.88% after hours to $3.96.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Unit Corporation declares $1.25 dividend
data.description:

data.title: Freddie Mac announces sale via auction of ~$290M worth of non-performing loans
data.description:
• Federal Home Loan Mortgage (OTCQB:FMCC) announced on Friday a sale via auction of ~$290M worth of non-performing loans.
  • The NPLs consist of seasoned, deeply delinquent residential first-lien whole loans held in the organization's mortgage-related investments portfolio.

data.title: Modine announces $100M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Modine (NYSE:MOD) on Friday announced a share repurchase program to repurchase up to $100 million of the company's outstanding common stock.
  • Source: Press Release
